commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer review of the draft PR and whether this narrow mitigation is enough for the linked issue scope.; Cleared: The diff changes Z.AI auth validation, auth-doctor output, and tests only; I found no new dependency, workflow, permission, secret exposure, or code-execution surface. (sha=ad552236f4bae6473f1aa3a6e6326a1942d91b72)
commands.review_summary:
The PR rejects Z.AI onboarding command text in interactive and non-interactive API-key auth, then adds doctor guidance and regression tests for stored Z.AI API-key profiles containing that command text.

PR surface: Source +64, Tests +93. Total +157 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the local root cause at source level: current main can accept and store non-empty command text as a Z.AI API key, and the linked issue supplies the polluted profile plus 401 logs. I did not reproduce a live Z.AI network call in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer review of the draft PR and whether this narrow mitigation is enough for the linked issue scope.

target.title: fix(zai): reject onboarding command auth profiles
target.body:
﻿Related: #97313

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Z.AI users could end up with an auth profile whose stored API key is the onboarding command text, such as `openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global`, causing provider calls to use that command string as the bearer token instead of the real key.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix keeps the repair narrow to the Z.AI auth boundary: Z.AI interactive and non-interactive auth now reject onboarding command text before it can be stored in `zai:default`, and auth doctor guidance identifies existing Z.AI profiles that already contain that bad command value.

After ClawSweeper review, the doctor guidance is routed through the actual static API-key health path in `noteAuthProfileHealthForTarget`, not only through the OAuth/token hint formatter. That means `openclaw doctor`'s auth health surface can report polluted Z.AI API-key profiles as `static [invalid_api_key]`.

This does not change sub-agent fallback behavior or the general auth profile precedence rules.

## User Impact

Users get an immediate setup error if they paste an OpenClaw onboarding command where a Z.AI API key is expected, and existing polluted `zai:default` profiles receive a clearer re-authentication hint instead of repeatedly failing as an opaque Z.AI 401.

## Evidence

Red/green proof from the initial fix:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before the fix, the new regression tests failed because Z.AI returned a profile with `key: "openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global"` and doctor returned an empty hint. After the fix: 2 files passed, 19 tests passed.

Review-fix regression proof:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before routing static Z.AI API-key profiles through `noteAuthProfileHealthForTarget`, the new regression failed with `Number of calls: 0`. After the fix, the focused suite passed in an earlier run: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed. A later rerun on this Windows worktree hung during Rolldown/Vitest initialization and was stopped; it emitted only repeated `[PLUGIN_TIMINGS]` warnings and no test failures.

Combined focused proof before the latest rerun hang:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Result: 3 files passed, 25 tests passed.

Fresh source-level doctor-output proof after the review fix:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import { saveAuthProfileStore } from './src/agents/auth-profiles/store.ts'; import { noteAuthProfileHealth } from './src/commands/doctor-auth.ts'; saveAuthProfileStore({ version: 1, profiles: { 'zai:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'zai', key: 'openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global' } } }, process.env.OPENCLAW_AGENT_DIR); await noteAuthProfileHealth({ cfg: { agents: { list: [{ id: 'main', default: true, agentDir: process.env.OPENCLAW_AGENT_DIR }] } }, prompter: { confirmAutoFix: async () => false }, allowKeychainPrompt: false });"
```

Output excerpt:

```text
o  Model auth ----------------------------------------------------------+
|  - zai:default: static [invalid_api_key] - Z.AI auth profile          |
|    zai:default contains an onboarding command instead of an API key.  |
|    Re-authenticate with a real key: openclaw onboard --auth-choice    |
|    zai-coding-global.                                                 |
```

Fresh lightweight checks after the review fix:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import assert from 'node:assert/strict'; import { formatStaticAuthProfileDoctorIssueLines } from './src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts'; const lines = formatStaticAuthProfileDoctorIssueLines({ store: { version: 1, profiles: { 'zai:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'zai', key: 'openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global' } } } }); assert.deepEqual(lines, ['- zai:default: static [invalid_api_key] - Z.AI auth profile zai:default contains an onboarding command instead of an API key. Re-authenticate with a real key: openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global.']); console.log('static doctor issue assertion passed');"
npx oxfmt --check extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts
npx oxlint extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts
git diff --check
```

All exited 0.

Full source CLI attempt:

```text
node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --non-interactive
```

This did not complete on this Windows worktree; it was stopped after more than two minutes with no doctor output beyond source-runner build activity. The source-level doctor-output proof above exercises the same exported auth doctor path with the real terminal `note` renderer.

Validation note: I do not have a live Z.AI / GLM-5.2 credential, so this PR proves the local auth-profile root cause with seeded/plugin/doctor-path evidence rather than a live network call.
target.author: lzy3538

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
